views.name: dashboard
views.hint: ASCII bar charts and sparklines, key=value metrics table
views.implementation: ASCII art banner hero, 4 metric bars with fill indicators, key=value system stats, 2 ASCII sparklines, tail-style alert log

views.name: projects
views.implementation: Tabular ls output with permissions, status badges, modified dates, file sizes, and directory names

views.name: project-workspace
views.implementation: Two vertical panes separated by tmux status line. Top pane shows project README, bottom pane shows shell with git log and test results

views.name: kanban
views.implementation: Eisenhower matrix grid with Urgent/Not Urgent columns and Important/Not Important rows. Single-line task entries with status codes

views.name: whiteboard
views.implementation: Network security architecture diagram using +--+ boxes and | pipes for connections, with a threat model flow from Internet to internal services

views.name: schema-planner
views.implementation: Three SQL CREATE TABLE statements for threat_indicators, incident_reports, and analysts tables with typed columns and constraints

views.name: directory-tree
views.hint: tree command output with box-drawing characters
views.implementation: Go project structure with +-- branch connectors, directory highlighting, file names, and size annotations

views.name: ideas
views.implementation: 10 numbered ideas with title, one-line summary, category tags, vote counts, and dates

views.name: ai-chat
views.implementation: User prompts after $ symbol, AI responses prefixed with =>, no chat bubbles, left-border accent on responses

views.name: settings
views.implementation: 4 INI sections (GENERAL, NOTIFICATIONS, SECURITY, SCANNING, APPEARANCE) with clickable [ON]/[OFF] toggles and cycling option values

libraries.name: Typed.js
libraries.usage: Available for typewriter text effects. Custom JS handles boot sequence and status bar typing for tighter terminal aesthetic control.

antiRepeatCompliance: No paper/red/light palette — uses black/green CRT
No Anton display font — uses monospace throughout
No newspaper/manifesto layout — uses terminal/CLI layout
No memo-format chat — uses REPL terminal chat
No swimlane kanban — uses Eisenhower priority matrix
